---
title: "Release catalogue"
subtitle: "BNR info-hub Technical Manual"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
---

# Release catalogue

## Purpose

This page explains the role of the BNR release catalogue.

The release catalogue is the public index of approved or publication-ready BNR analytical products.

It is not just a file list. It explains what has been released, what period it covers, what files are available, and where users can find the web briefing, PDF, tables, figure data, metadata, and build records.

## Location

The release catalogue lives here:

```text
site/downloads/release-catalogue.qmd
```

## Why the catalogue exists

As the site grows, users need one place to answer:

- What has BNR published?
- Which version is current?
- What does the product cover?
- Where is the PDF?
- Where are the CSV tables?
- Where is the metadata?
- Has this release been superseded?

## Minimum entry fields

Each release entry should include:

| Field | Meaning |
|---|---|
| title | public-facing product title |
| briefing ID | stable release identifier |
| product type | static briefing, annual report, dashboard data, etc. |
| domain | CVD, primary care, cancer, etc. |
| period | target year or reporting period |
| baseline | comparison period, if relevant |
| status | draft, approved, superseded, archived |
| compute layer | usually Stata |
| publication layer | usually Quarto |

## Links to include

For each static briefing, include links to:

- web briefing
- PDF briefing
- public CSV tables
- figure data CSVs
- figure image files
- metadata
- build record
- ZIP bundle, if created later

## Status definitions

| Status | Meaning |
|---|---|
| Draft / pilot | product is being tested and may still be revised |
| Approved | product has been reviewed and approved for public release |
| Superseded | product remains available but has been replaced by a later version |
| Archived | product is retained for historical reference only |

## Manual first, metadata-driven later

For the first few briefings, the catalogue can be manually maintained in QMD.

Later, the catalogue may be generated from YAML or CSV metadata if the release volume grows.

:::{.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Why not automate this immediately?

A manual catalogue entry is easier to inspect while the workflow is still being stabilised.

Once several products follow the same structure, a metadata-driven catalogue may become worthwhile.
:::

## Example briefing ID

```text
cvd_cases_2023_v1
```

This means:

- `cvd` = cardiovascular disease
- `cases` = case-count briefing
- `2023` = target year
- `v1` = release version

## Release catalogue checklist

Before marking a release as approved, confirm that:

- the web briefing link works
- the PDF link works
- all CSV download links work
- metadata and build files are present
- status is correct
- no private paths or confidential files are exposed
